source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure to Implement Monitoring and Alerting

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure to Implement Monitoring and Alerting
input_row.description: This helps by alerting the system if any unusual event has occurred or if a particular threshold has been achieved because the user is able to set a desired interval or the cluster. This then allows system administrators to swiftly correct the situation and avoid subsequent complications if something unusual is happening.
input_row.rationale: Impact: Has a positive impact in the business operations when the issue is identified and addressed accordingly.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign into the AWS Management Console • Sign into the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the Amazon DocumentDB Console • Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/docdb/. 3. Select the DocumentDB Cluster • Choose the Amazon DocumentDB cluster for which you want to implement monitoring and alerting. • Click on the cluster name to access its details page. • In the cluster details page, navigate to the "Monitoring" section. 4. Enable Enhanced Monitoring • Under the Enhanced Monitoring section. • Click on the Edit button or Modify option to configure enhanced monitoring settings. • Enable the desired metrics and set the desired monitoring interval for the cluster. • Enhanced monitoring provides additional insights into the performance and health of your DocumentDB cluster.  5. Set Up CloudWatch Alarms • Scroll down to the CloudWatch Alarms section. • Click on the Create alarm button. • Configure the CloudWatch alarm based on the metrics you want to monitor and the thresholds you want to set. • Specify the actions to be taken when the alarm is triggered, such as sending notifications or executing automated actions. 6. Customize Metrics and Dashboards (Optional) • If desired, you can customize the metrics and dashboards in Amazon CloudWatch to suit your specific monitoring requirements. • Create custom metrics, build personalized dashboards, and set up alarms based on your application's unique needs. 7. Test Monitoring and Alerting • Perform operations on your DocumentDB cluster to generate metric data and trigger the configured alarms. • Verify that CloudWatch is capturing the metrics and triggering the appropriate actions based on your alarm settings. 8. Regularly Review and Fine-Tune • Regularly review the monitoring metrics, CloudWatch alarms, and any event- driven actions triggered by DocumentDB events. • Fine-tune the monitoring settings, alarms, and notifications based on the observed patterns and requirements of your application.
input_row.impact: Has a positive impact in the business operations when the issue is identified and addressed accordingly.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control involves enabling and configuring monitoring and alerting features for Amazon DocumentDB clusters, which are standard properties of AWS services. These settings can be verified using AWS APIs because AWS provides APIs to access and configure monitoring and alerting through CloudWatch and DocumentDB. This audit does not require human judgment, SSH access, or application-level checks, making it suitable for automation.

✓ Can AWS APIs query this property? → Yes, via CloudWatch and DocumentDB APIs
✓ Is this a standard AWS service property (encryption, logging, access control, network rules)? → Yes, monitoring and alerting fall under standard service configurations
✓ Does boto3 SDK have methods to check this? → Yes, methods exist to query CloudWatch alarms and DocumentDB settings
✓ Does AWS CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Yes, CLI commands can be used to describe CloudWatch alarms and DocumentDB clusters

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_docdb_cluster_monitoring_and_alerting_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use `boto3` to interact with both CloudWatch and DocumentDB services.
- Use `boto3` methods like `describe_db_clusters` to list all DocumentDB clusters and check the configuration of enhanced monitoring.
- Use `boto3` methods like `describe_alarms` from the CloudWatch client to verify that alarms are set up for critical DocumentDB metrics.
- Enumerate all regions to ensure no clusters are missed.
- Check settings for enhanced monitoring intervals and alert thresholds.
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - Ensure the DocumentDB cluster has enhanced monitoring enabled.
  - Ensure CloudWatch alarms are configured for relevant metrics with actions specified for when thresholds are breached.
- Example: Use `boto3` to verify that `DBClusterParameterGroup` and `CloudWatch Alarms` are correctly configured for metrics like CPU utilization, storage space, and connections. Ensure actions like notifications or automated responses are specified when alarms are triggered.
